subject: Reasoning Ability
question: In a certain code language, GRAPES is written as 66. How will PRAISE be written in that language?
q: In a certain code language, GRAPES is written as 66. How will PRAISE be written in that language?
explanation: $Sum \text{ of } positional values \text{ of } GRAPES (7+18+1+16+5+19) = 66. Similarly for PRAISE, (16+18+1+9+19+5) = 68.$

BZE, CWG, ESI, ?, LHM
q:
Select the lettercluster from among the given options that can replace the question mark (?) in the following series.
BZE, CWG, ESI, ?, LHM
explanation: First letter: B(+1)C(+2)E(+3)H(+4)L. Second letter: Z(3)W(4)S(5)N(6)H. Third letter: E(+2)G(+2)I(+2)K(+2)M. So missing cluster is HNK.

explanation: TVX (+2, +2), BDF (+2, +2), PRT (+2, +2) follow +2 pattern. GJL (+3, +2) has +3, making it the odd one out.

subject: Reasoning Ability
question: Seven friends are sitting around a circular table facing the centre. A is sitting second to the left of B. C is sitting between B and G. D is sitting second to the left of C. E, who is sitting second to the right of G, is sitting between A and F. Who is sitting second to the left of G?
q: Seven friends are sitting around a circular table facing the centre. A is sitting second to the left of B. C is sitting between B and G. D is sitting second to the left of C. E, who is sitting second to the right of G, is sitting between A and F. Who is sitting second to the left of G?
explanation: Arranging the 7 friends in order around the circle shows that B sits second to the left of G.

subject: Reasoning Ability
question: In a certain code language, 'NOSE' is written as 'QPVF' and 'HAND' is written as 'KBQE'. How will 'FACE' be written in that language?
q: In a certain code language, 'NOSE' is written as 'QPVF' and 'HAND' is written as 'KBQE'. How will 'FACE' be written in that language?
explanation: Pattern: N(+3)Q, O(+1)P, S(+3)V, E(+1)F (+3, +1, +3, +1). Applying to FACE gives F(+3)I, A(+1)B, C(+3)F, E(+1)F ⇒ IBFF.

explanation: Arrangement from West to East facing South is L, K, N, M. The friend sitting to the immediate left of K (facing South) is N.

subject: Reasoning Ability
question:
Read the given statements and conclusions carefully. Assuming that the information given in the statements is true, even if it appears to be at variance with commonly known facts, decide which of the given conclusions logically follow(s) from the statements.

Statements:
All lyricists are morningwalkers.
No accountant is a graduate.
All morningwalkers are accountants.

Conclusions:
I. No lyricist is a graduate.
II. Some lyricists are graduates.
q:
Read the given statements and conclusions carefully. Assuming that the information given in the statements is true, even if it appears to be at variance with commonly known facts, decide which of the given conclusions logically follow(s) from the statements.

Statements:
All lyricists are morningwalkers.
No accountant is a graduate.
All morningwalkers are accountants.

Conclusions:
I. No lyricist is a graduate.
II. Some lyricists are graduates.

options.text: Neither conclusion I nor II follows
Either conclusion I or II follows

explanation: Since all lyricists are morningwalkers and all morningwalkers are accountants, all lyricists are accountants. Since no accountant is a graduate, no lyricist can be a graduate. Thus, Conclusion I follows.

subject: Reasoning Ability
question: In a joint family, there are 8 members P, Q, R, S, T, U, V and W. Q is the brother of S. V is the son of Q and R. S is the brother of U. W is the daughter of S and T. P is the wife of V. How is R related to P?
q: In a joint family, there are 8 members P, Q, R, S, T, U, V and W. Q is the brother of S. V is the son of Q and R. S is the brother of U. W is the daughter of S and T. P is the wife of V. How is R related to P?
explanation: Q and R are parents of V. P is V's wife. Therefore, R is the motherinlaw of P.

subject: Quantitative Aptitude
question: A dairy owner sells 528 litres of milk daily in onelitre packets of 3 varieties: Full Cream, Toned, and Double Toned. Sales of Toned milk packets are 80% more than Full Cream milk packets, and sales of Double Toned milk packets are 60% less than Toned milk packets. How many packets of Double Toned milk does he sell daily?
q: A dairy owner sells 528 litres of milk daily in onelitre packets of 3 varieties: Full Cream, Toned, and Double Toned. Sales of Toned milk packets are 80% more than Full Cream milk packets, and sales of Double Toned milk packets are 60% less than Toned milk packets. How many packets of Double Toned milk does he sell daily?
explanation: $Let Full Cream = x packets. Toned = 1.8x. Double Toned = 0.4(1.8x) = 0.72x. Total = 3.52x = 528 ⇒ x = 150. Double Toned = 0.72 × 150 = 108 packets.$

subject: Quantitative Aptitude
question: If bananas are sold at ₹10 per banana instead of ₹7 per banana, the difference between the selling prices of some bananas is ₹990. If the cost price of all bananas is ₹1,980, and if the profit earned is 18%, what will be the selling price (in ₹) of ten bananas?
q: If bananas are sold at ₹10 per banana instead of ₹7 per banana, the difference between the selling prices of some bananas is ₹990. If the cost price of all bananas is ₹1,980, and if the profit earned is 18%, what will be the selling price (in ₹) of ten bananas?
explanation: $Difference per banana = ₹10 - ₹7 = ₹3. Number \text{ of } bananas = 990 / 3 = 330. Total CP = ₹1980. SP with 18\% profit = 1980 × 1.18 = ₹2336.40 for 330 bananas. SP \text{ of } 10 bananas = 2336.40 / 33 = ₹70.80.$

subject: Quantitative Aptitude
question: To complete a job, B takes five times the number of days taken by A. Working together, they complete the work in 15 days. How many days will A alone take to complete the same work?
q: To complete a job, B takes five times the number of days taken by A. Working together, they complete the work in 15 days. How many days will A alone take to complete the same work?
explanation: $Let A's efficiency = 5, B's efficiency = 1. Combined efficiency = 6. Total work = 6 × 15 = 90 units. Days taken by A alone = 90 / 5 = 18 days.$

subject: Quantitative Aptitude
question: A, B and C are three numbers. A is 45% more than B and C is 60% less than A. By what percentage (nearest integer) is B greater than C?
q: A, B and C are three numbers. A is 45% more than B and C is 60% less than A. By what percentage (nearest integer) is B greater than C?
explanation: $Let B = 100. Then A = 145. C = 0.40 × 145 = 58. Percentage by which B is greater than C = [(100-58) / 58] × 100 = 72.4\% ≈ 72\%.$

subject: Quantitative Aptitude
question: Which is the smallest number that is divisible by all numbers from 4 to 9?
q: Which is the smallest number that is divisible by all numbers from 4 to 9?
explanation: The smallest number divisible by 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9 is their LCM, which is 2520.

subject: Quantitative Aptitude
question: A wall 18 m long, 10 m high and 40 cm wide is to be constructed using bricks of dimensions 30 cm, 15 cm and 10 cm. How many bricks (in thousands) will be required?
q: A wall 18 m long, 10 m high and 40 cm wide is to be constructed using bricks of dimensions 30 cm, 15 cm and 10 cm. How many bricks (in thousands) will be required?
explanation: $Volume \text{ of } wall = 1800 × 1000 × 40 cm^3. Volume \text{ of } brick = 30 × 15 × 10 cm^3. Number \text{ of } bricks = (1800 × 1000 × 40) / (30 × 15 × 10) = 16,000 bricks = 16 thousand.$

subject: Quantitative Aptitude
question: Find the difference between the average of the first three multiples of 3 between 10 and 20 and the average of the first two multiples of 4.
q: Find the difference between the average of the first three multiples of 3 between 10 and 20 and the average of the first two multiples of 4.
explanation: $Multiples \text{ of } 3 between 10 and 20 are 12, 15, 18. Average = 15. First two multiples \text{ of } 4 are 4, 8 (or 4, 8; avg 6) or multiples 12, 16 (avg 14). Difference = 15-14 = 1.$

subject: Quantitative Aptitude
question: What is the value of 10 − [378 ÷ 6 of 9 − (25 − 72 ÷ 3)]?
q: What is the value of 10 − [378 ÷ 6 of 9 − (25 − 72 ÷ 3)]?

subject: Quantitative Aptitude
question: The compound interest received on a sum of ₹75,000 in 2 years at a certain annual compound interest rate compounded annually is ₹6,120. If interest is compounded quarterly, what will be the amount received on ₹1,00,000 at the same interest rate after one year (in ₹, correct to two decimal places)?
q: The compound interest received on a sum of ₹75,000 in 2 years at a certain annual compound interest rate compounded annually is ₹6,120. If interest is compounded quarterly, what will be the amount received on ₹1,00,000 at the same interest rate after one year (in ₹, correct to two decimal places)?
explanation: $CI = 6120 on 75000 in 2 yrs ⇒ Rate = 4\% p.a. Compounded quarterly at 1\% per quarter for 4 quarters on ₹1,00,000 gives Amount = 100000(1.01)^4 = ₹1,04,060.40.$

subject: Quantitative Aptitude
question: What is the value of 72 − [20 − {10 − (6 − 6 − 4)}]?
q: What is the value of 72 − [20 − {10 − (6 − 6 − 4)}]?

subject: Quantitative Aptitude
question: A person crosses a 1000 m long bridge in 6 minutes. What is his speed (in km/h)?
q: A person crosses a 1000 m long bridge in 6 minutes. What is his speed (in km/h)?
explanation: $Speed = Distance / Time = 1000 m / 360 s = 2.777 m/s = 2.777 × 3.6 = 10 km/h.$

subject: Quantitative Aptitude
question: A certain sum of money at uniform annual simple interest rate amounts to ₹12,420 in 4 years and ₹14,985 in 7 years. What is the sum?
q: A certain sum of money at uniform annual simple interest rate amounts to ₹12,420 in 4 years and ₹14,985 in 7 years. What is the sum?
explanation: $Interest for 3 years = 14985-12420 = ₹2565 ⇒ Interest per year = ₹855. Principal = 12420 - (4 × 855) = ₹9,000.$

subject: Quantitative Aptitude
question: Find the ratio of the Least Common Multiple (LCM) of the first four prime numbers to the Highest Common Factor (HCF) of the first three composite numbers.
q: Find the ratio of the Least Common Multiple (LCM) of the first four prime numbers to the Highest Common Factor (HCF) of the first three composite numbers.
explanation: $First four prime numbers: 2, 3, 5, 7 ⇒ LCM = 210. First three composite numbers: 4, 6, 8 ⇒ HCF = 2. Ratio = 210 : 2 = 105 : 1.$

subject: Quantitative Aptitude
question: $If A : B = 8 : 9 and B : C = 12 : 55, then what is the value \text{ of } A : B : C?$

subject: Quantitative Aptitude
question: Shopkeeper A gives two successive discounts of 8% and 12% on a marked price of ₹500. Another shopkeeper B gives a single discount of 20% on the same article with the same marked price. Find the difference in their selling prices.
q: Shopkeeper A gives two successive discounts of 8% and 12% on a marked price of ₹500. Another shopkeeper B gives a single discount of 20% on the same article with the same marked price. Find the difference in their selling prices.
explanation: $Shopkeeper A SP = 500 × 0.92 × 0.88 = ₹404.80. Shopkeeper B SP = 500 × 0.80 = ₹400.00. Difference = ₹4.80.$

subject: Quantitative Aptitude
question: Two trains A and B of equal length are running in the same direction on parallel tracks at speeds of 64.8 km/h and 50.4 km/h respectively. Train A crosses Train B in one minute. Find the length of each train (in m).
q: Two trains A and B of equal length are running in the same direction on parallel tracks at speeds of 64.8 km/h and 50.4 km/h respectively. Train A crosses Train B in one minute. Find the length of each train (in m).
explanation: $Relative speed = 64.8-50.4 = 14.4 km/h = 4 m/s. Distance covered in 60 s = 2 × Length = 4 × 60 = 240 m ⇒ Length \text{ of } each train = 120 m.$

subject: Quantitative Aptitude
question: A and B together can complete a work in 24 days. B and C together can complete it in 30 days. If A's efficiency is twice that of C, how many days will B alone take to complete the same work?
q: A and B together can complete a work in 24 days. B and C together can complete it in 30 days. If A's efficiency is twice that of C, how many days will B alone take to complete the same work?
explanation: $Work efficiencies: A + B = 1/24, B + C = 1/30. Given A = 2C ⇒ (2C + B) - (B + C) = 1/24-1/30 ⇒ C = 1/120. B = 1/30-1/120 = 3/120 = 1/40. Days taken by B alone = 40 days.$

subject: Quantitative Aptitude
question: $The perimeter \text{ of } a circular plot is 440 m. Around it, there is a circular path 5 m wide. What is the area \text{ of } the path (in m^2, taking pi = 22/7) approximately?$

subject: Quantitative Aptitude
question: A sum of ₹7,800 is divided among A, B and C such that B's share is 1/3 of A's share and C's share is 20% more than B's share. Find A's share.
q: A sum of ₹7,800 is divided among A, B and C such that B's share is 1/3 of A's share and C's share is 20% more than B's share. Find A's share.

subject: Quantitative Aptitude
question: The average weight of 23 students was recorded as 53 kg. On including the weight of a teacher, the average weight increased by 100 g. Find the weight of the teacher (in kg).
q: The average weight of 23 students was recorded as 53 kg. On including the weight of a teacher, the average weight increased by 100 g. Find the weight of the teacher (in kg).
explanation: $Total weight \text{ of } 23 students = 23 × 53 = 1219 kg. Total weight \text{ of } 24 people = 24 × 53.1 = 1274.4 kg. Teacher's weight = 1274.4-1219 = 55.4 kg.$

subject: English Language
question:
Comprehension:
In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank.

The human skeleton is the (1)______ framework of our body. It gives shape and support to the body. (2)______, it provides protection, makes blood cells, stores minerals and regulates hormones. The spinal cord is an (3)______ part of our body. The ribcage protects our lungs and heart. To increase the (4)______ of our bones, we should include calcium in our diet. All our (5)______ organs are protected by this skeletal system.

SubQuestion No : 1
Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank no. 1.
